— GPC, Anthracite Coal & Electrode Scrap — Supplied to Indian Steel & Foundry Industry
Low Sulphur · Low Nitrogen · All sizes 0–30mm · EAF · Induction Furnace · Foundry Grade
"Enquire for Supply / Get a Quote" — Indian steel plants and foundries
"Offer Your Carbon Raiser Supply" — for Chinese and global exporters
with every lot · SGS inspection available · Ex-stock at Indian ports
or carbon additive) is a high-carbon material added to molten metal during steelmaking or casting to precisely control and restore the carbon content of the final product.

During melting in an EAF, induction furnace or cupola, carbon is lost as the iron is refined. If final carbon is too low, the steel or casting will be weak, soft and brittle. A carbon raiser corrects this — accurately and efficiently.
Four parameters that define quality — at a glance:
Higher = more actual carbon reaching the melt
Excess S causes hot shortness in steel; prevents
Excess N causes porosity and blowholes — critical for ductile iron
Higher = less material needed per heat, less slag, lower real cost
Quick comparison — full specifications on each dedicated product page:
Ductile iron, special steel, low S/N grades
full specifications, applications and technical details of each grade — see the dedicated product pages linked below.
EAF steel plants — trim carbon after refining; both GPC (quality steel) and anthracite (commodity grades) used
Induction furnace units — 600+ IF units across
Grey iron foundries — control carbon equivalent (CE) in cupola and induction furnaces
Ductile iron foundries — GPC preferred; low S and N essential before magnesium treatment
Special steel manufacturers — ultra-low S/N grades require premium GPC only
All three grades from one supplier — simplified procurement, one COA standard
Fixed origin source — consistent chemistry lot to lot, no surprise variations
Full COA per lot: F.C., S, N, Ash, VM, Moisture, size analysis
SGS / Intertek inspection at origin available on request
for trial orders — no full container needed
Container orders 20–24 MT — all major sizes
Payment on committed timelines — reliable, transparent settlement

We actively import GPC, calcined anthracite and electrode scrap for the Indian market. We evaluate suppliers on actual F.C., S,
per lot COA — not just headline specifications.
Supplier form fields: Company · Country · Product type · F.C. % · S % · N % · Monthly capacity (MT) · FOB price · Contact
Q1. What is a carbon raiser and why
During melting, carbon is lost from molten metal. A carbon raiser restores it to the exact target percentage required for the steel or casting grade
Q2. Which carbon raiser has the best absorption rate?
GPC leads at 90–95%, followed by electrode scrap at 88–94% and anthracite at 75–85%. Higher absorption means less material per heat and lower actual cost per unit of carbon delivered.
Q3. Why does nitrogen matter in a carbon raiser?
In ductile iron, nitrogen above 80–100 ppm causes sub-surface porosity and poor nodule formation. GPC at 0.003–0.03% N is the only
that reliably keeps nitrogen low enough for quality ductile iron.
Q4. What particle size should I specify?
1–5mm is standard for EAF and induction furnace use — fast dissolution with minimal fines loss.
mm suits ladle trim additions; 3–8mm suits large EAF or cupola operations.
Q5. What is the minimum order and
for trials; standard container is 20–24 MT. Ex-stock grades deliver in 3–7 days after customs clearance; fresh import from China takes 30–40 days.
